evals.prompt: The checkout function in billing/checkout.py has grown into a 200-line mess. Extract the tax and discount logic into their own functions to clean it up. It has decent test coverage.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the existing tests first to confirm green, extracts tax and discount into separate functions in small steps re-running tests after each, preserves observable behavior exactly (no bug fixes or behavior tweaks folded in), and commits the extraction as a refactor-only change.
assertions: The agent runs the existing tests before starting and confirms they pass
The extraction preserves observable behavior with no feature, fix, or behavior change mixed in
The work proceeds in small steps with tests run between them, not one large edit verified only at the end
The refactor is committed on its own, described as a no-behavior-change refactor

evals.prompt: Refactor the legacy parseConfig function in config/parse.go. It has no tests at all and the control flow is a rat's nest of nested ifs. Make it readable.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes the code is untested and writes characterization tests that pin down the current behavior (bug-for-bug) before restructuring, then refactors in small steps keeping those tests green, rather than restructuring blind and asserting behavior is unchanged.
assertions: The agent adds characterization tests capturing current behavior before restructuring the code
The characterization tests assert what the code does today, including quirks, not an idealized contract
The restructuring is done in small steps with the new tests kept green throughout
No behavior change is introduced under the refactor, or if a bug is spotted it is called out for a separate change rather than fixed in place

evals.prompt: While you're renaming the User.load method to User.fetch across the codebase, I noticed it doesn't handle the case where the ID is null and it probably should return an error there. Fix that too while you're in there.
evals.expected_output: The agent keeps the rename (a pure refactor) separate from the null-handling behavior change: the rename lands as its own commit with no behavior change, and the null-ID error handling lands as a separate commit with a test, rather than bundling both into one diff.
assertions: The rename is kept as a behavior-preserving refactor with no logic changes mixed in
The null-ID handling is treated as a separate behavior change, committed apart from the rename
The behavior change ships with a test demonstrating the new null handling
The agent does not fold the fix into the rename commit or diff

evals.prompt: This payment retry module is ugly and I hate looking at it. Rewrite it from scratch to be cleaner. It's the code path that charges customer cards in production.
evals.expected_output: The agent resists a big-bang rewrite of a critical untested-or-lightly-tested path, instead ensuring a test safety net exists and reshaping incrementally while keeping the suite green, or building a replacement beside the original and migrating call sites one at a time rather than deleting and rewriting in one edit.
assertions: The agent does not delete and rewrite the module in one big-bang edit that is never green in between
The agent establishes or confirms a test safety net before restructuring the critical path
Any replacement is introduced incrementally, keeping the code building and tests green throughout
Behavior of the payment path is preserved, with structural cleanup separated from any behavior change
